Title: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
Subhead: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

Title: EU agrees to implement long-delayed trade pact with Washington
Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
'Heat strokes are part of the job': In Delhi, construction workers endure 43°C heat
Subhead: India's capital has faced scorching days since April. On construction sites, workers have tried to adapt but many have fallen victim to heatstroke.

Title: Putin and Xi reaffirm 'unyielding' ties days after Trump visit
Subhead: On his visit to China, Vladimir Putin told Xi Jinping that relations between the two countries had reached an 'unprecedentedly high level' despite 'unfavorable external factors,' without naming any third country.

Title: Cannes 2026: 'Moulin' brings the ordeal of a French Resistance hero harrowingly to life
Subhead: Laszlo Nemes chose to focus his film on the confrontation between French resistance hero Jean Moulin and Klaus Barbie, the head of the Lyon Gestapo, using an immersive approach that raises questions.

Title: Macron's nominee for central bank faces crunch vote in increasingly hostile parliament
Subhead: Emmanuel Moulin, candidate for governor of France's central bank, is set to face hearings before two parliamentary finance committees on Wednesday. Ahead of the 2027 presidential election, the procedure is no longer a mere formality.

The EU's unflinching collaboration with Libya and Tunisia to contain the flow of migrants
Subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (2/4). Giorgia Meloni's government, backed by the European Union, is funding, equipping and training both the Libyan and Tunisian coast guards – even though these forces have committed rights abuses – to intercept people in exile on their way to Europe.

Title: Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
Subhead: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

Title: Trump's ballroom obsession worries Republicans
Subhead: The costly and monumental real estate project has repeatedly been mentioned in presidential speeches, highlighting the billionaire's disconnect from public concerns. Only 28% of citizens deemed the construction necessary.

Title: Canal+ controversy undermines French cinema
'Patriarchy is based on the idea that women's and children's bodies are at men's disposal'
With generative AI, 'humankind has created, for the first time in its history, a being that mimics it in its very essence: the ability to think, to create, to decide'
